headline: Anthropic pushed Claude into Slack with a scoped agent identity, the week's clearest move toward governed multiplayer agents.
body: Claude Tag lets Claude operate in Slack channels under its own scoped identity rather than impersonating users, with admin governance, full auditability, and persistent context across days, available in beta on Team and Enterprise plans. This lands alongside a broader agentic-economy framing in which millions of autonomous agents transact and negotiate, raising automation bias and security concerns like dynamic cloaking and agentic traps. On the technique side, a cluster of new entrants around data backup, immutability, disaster recovery, and ransomware protection broke into the window, while power iteration registered as a breakout. Transformer fundamentals also resurfaced through a visual GPT walkthrough covering self-attention, feed-forward networks, layer normalization, and residual connections.

cited_items.title: Agent identity: A new access model for Claude Tag
Tag Claude in, right where you already work
When millions of AI agents meet
